purpose: Translate one bounded D2 batch of extracted source-language text units into the declared target language with terminology, layout variant, and coverage records. Full-run semantic translations are assembled only after all batches validate.
normalization_policy: Numeric metric labels must be normalized by target language using only current-unit text-shape evidence. Examples of allowed generic units are percentage/per cent/percent, basis points, percentage points, pps/bps, currency units, million/billion/trillion, months, and years. For target_language=zh, do not leave lower-case English metric words unchanged when a target-language symbol or unit can preserve the same numeric claim. This is not a sample-specific glossary and must not use file names, page numbers, reference PDFs, or document-specific constants.
system_prompt: You are a financial-report translation engine. Translate only supplied source units from source_language to target_language for the current batch. Preserve typed tokens such as years, percentages, currency amounts, dates, footnote markers, and table semantics. The target text must translate the actual source meaning, names, entities, relationships, and numeric claims. Neutral identifiers such as CUSIP, ISIN, SEDOL, RIC, LEI, ticker-like codes, rating letters, and uppercase alphanumeric security codes should normally be excluded by the batch manifest; if such a token appears inside a translated unit, preserve it as a neutral identifier instead of inventing a translation. Do not describe the line category, do not summarize as metadata, and do not emit boilerplate such as 'This line reports...', 'This line describes...', '本行说明...', '本行列示...', or '保留数值与标记...'. If a unit is a constrained label, compact label, legend, side navigation label, chart label, or table header, provide semantically tied shorter display variants in layout_variants; do not expect the PDF generator to invent abbreviations. Return strict JSON for this batch and do not omit units silently. If you cannot translate every unit in the batch, return FAIL and list missing_unit_ids; do not create placeholders.
user_prompt_template: Translate one D2 batch for run {{run_id}} in state {{state_id}}. Run mode: {{run_mode}}. Source PDF: {{source_pdf_ref}}. Source language: {{source_language}}. Target language: {{target_language}}. Target text field: {{target_text_field}}. Batch id: {{batch_id}}. Batch index/count: {{batch_index}}/{{batch_count}}. Batch slot values ref: {{batch_slot_values_ref}}. Translation units: {{translation_units}}. Terminology policy: {{terminology_policy}}. Before persisting prompt/model/decision artifacts, the executor must have produced a workspace boundary report at {{batch_workspace_boundary_ref}} with workspace_boundary_verdict=PASS for all planned batch output paths. Output one record per source unit in this batch only. Do not output placeholders or meta descriptions. Forbidden target patterns include 'This line reports/describes/lists...', '本行说明/列示/报告...', '当前页的财务报告、治理或业务信息', and any instruction text like 'preserve figures/markers' or '保留数值与标记'. Preserve typed tokens in preserve_tokens but do not mention the preservation instruction in the translation. Put the full semantic target text in {{target_text_field}} and translation_target_text. For units whose text_role/layout_hint is compact_label, short_label, table_cell, legend, vertical_nav, chart_label, or table_header, layout_variants is required and must include the matching target-language suffix keys such as compact_label_zh/compact_label_en, short_label_zh/short_label_en, table_cell_zh/table_cell_en, legend_zh/legend_en, vertical_nav_zh/vertical_nav_en, plus display_zh/display_en when useful. Variants must preserve the same concept as the full target text and may use domain-standard abbreviations from the supplied source/terminology only; do not invent document-specific shorthand. Persist the raw model output to {{batch_model_output_ref}} before validation.

slots.batch_slot_values_ref: path to batch slot_values JSON created by build_translation_batch_manifest.py
slots.batch_model_output_ref: path where raw batch model output must be persisted
slots.batch_workspace_boundary_ref: path to validate_workspace_boundary.py report for all planned prompt/model/decision/validation output paths in this batch
slots.translation_units: array(object(unit_id, page_index, region_id, source_text, bbox, text_role, optional layout_hint compact_label|short_label|table_cell|legend|vertical_nav|chart_label|table_header|body|footnote))

properties.translation_provider: model/provider identifier; deterministic_placeholder is forbidden in product_quality
properties.units: array(object(unit_id, page_index, source_text, translation_target_text, target field named by target_text_field such as translation_zh or translation_en, preserve_tokens, term_decisions, layout_risk, optional layout_variants object(compact_label_zh, short_label_zh, table_cell_zh, legend_zh, compact_zh, display_zh, compact_label_en, short_label_en, table_cell_en, legend_en, compact_en, display_en)))
properties.prompt_artifacts: array(object(prompt_instance, slot_values, model_output, batch_validation, decision_record, workspace_boundary))

batch_materialization_policy: D2 is a loop inside S5. build_translation_batch_manifest.py creates bounded batch slot files using the shared required-unit rule; excluded neutral/already-target-language visible lines are not semantic-coverage obligations. For each batch, validate_workspace_boundary.py must pass on planned prompt/model/decision/validation output paths before any runtime artifact write; validate_translation_manifest_boundaries.py may write the equivalent per-batch boundary reports from the manifest for large documents. This prompt fills only that batch. When materialize_d2_translation_batches.py is used, it must consume this template, write prompt_instance/model_output/decision_record, normalize request text with NFKC, validate cache hits against the target script, and record the provider. For target_language=en, CJK residue may be removed only by the tool's generic Latin-dominant mixed-identifier cleanup after translation; the model prompt must not use sample-specific replacements. Chunked provider calls are allowed only as an execution optimization inside one bounded batch: every source unit must be wrapped with a stable marker, split back into the original unit_id, recorded under chunk_translation evidence, and validated by validate_translation_batch.py. Failed marker split must fall back to per-unit translation or route to S_FAIL_CAPABILITY. validate_translation_batch.py must pass for each batch. assemble_semantic_translations.py creates docs/input/semantic_translations/<case_id>.translations.json only after all required batch outputs exist. validate_semantic_translations.py is the final S5 gate.
failure_policy: If any source unit in the current batch is not translated, if placeholders or line-category meta descriptions are used, or if provider/coverage metadata is missing, return FAIL with missing_unit_ids and invalid_unit_ids. Missing executable translation capacity routes to S_FAIL_CAPABILITY; do not fall back to placeholder generation.
anti_overfit_policy: Do not use known sample translations. Translate the supplied units only.
